questions.question: Let A = {1, 2, 3} and B = {a, b, c}. Find the number of elements in the Cartesian product A × B. (3 marks)
questions.hint: A × B means ordered pairs (x, y) where x ∈ A and y ∈ B

questions.question: Prove that the Cartesian product of two sets A and B × C is equal to the Cartesian product of A and B × C, i.e., (A × B) × C = A × (B × C). (5 marks)
questions.hint: Use the definition of Cartesian product and properties of sets

questions.question: Find the number of ordered pairs (x, y) in the Cartesian product A × B where x ∈ A = {1, 2, 3} and y ∈ B = {a, b, c}, such that x = 2 and y ≠ b. (5 marks)
questions.hint: Use set notation and basic set operations
